commit.message:
Enforce non-null `fShape` invariant in `G4OCCTSolid` (#185)

* Initial plan

* Enforce non-null fShape invariant in G4OCCTSolid

The constructor and SetOCCTShape() now throw std::invalid_argument when
given a null TopoDS_Shape, establishing a class invariant that fShape is
always non-null. All defensive fShape.IsNull() guards in method bodies
are removed as dead code. Documentation updated throughout to reflect the
invariant. Tests added for null-shape rejection; regression test updated
to use an empty compound shape in place of the now-rejected null shape.

- Use braced initializer lists for return statements (modernize-return-braced-init-list)

commit.message:
fix: ensure vision jam always targets active non-self player

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cli): embed core pack in wheel for offline/air-gapped deployment (#1803)

* feat(cli): embed core pack in wheel + offline-first init (#1711, #1752)

Bundle templates, commands, and scripts inside the specify-cli wheel so
that `specify init` works without any network access by default.

Changes:
- pyproject.toml: add hatchling force-include for core_pack assets; bump
  version to 0.2.1
- __init__.py: add _locate_core_pack(), _generate_agent_commands() (Python
  port of generate_commands() shell function), and scaffold_from_core_pack();
  modify init() to scaffold from bundled assets by default; add --from-github
  flag to opt back in to the GitHub download path
- release.yml: build wheel during CI release job
- create-github-release.sh: attach .whl as a release asset
- docs/installation.md: add Enterprise/Air-Gapped Installation section
- README.md: add Option 3 enterprise install with accurate offline story

Closes #1711
Addresses #1752

* fix(tests): update kiro alias test for offline-first scaffold path

* feat(cli): invoke bundled release script at runtime for offline scaffold

- Embed release scripts (bash + PowerShell) in wheel via pyproject.toml
- Replace Python _generate_agent_commands() with subprocess invocation of
  the canonical create-release-packages.sh, guaranteeing byte-for-byte
  parity between 'specify init --offline' and GitHub release ZIPs
- Fix macOS bash 3.2 compat in release script: replace cp --parents,
  local -n (nameref), and mapfile with POSIX-safe alternatives
- Fix _TOML_AGENTS: remove qwen (uses markdown per release script)
- Rename --from-github to --offline (opt-in to bundled assets)
- Add _locate_release_script() for cross-platform script discovery
- Update tests: remove bash 4+/GNU coreutils requirements, handle
  Kimi directory-per-skill layout, 576 tests passing
- Update CHANGELOG and docs/installation.md

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(offline): error out if --offline fails instead of falling back to network

- _locate_core_pack() docstring now accurately describes that it only
  finds wheel-bundled core_pack/; source-checkout fallback lives in callers
- init() --offline + no bundled assets now exits with a clear error
  (previously printed a warning and silently fell back to GitHub download)
- init() scaffold failure under --offline now exits with an error
  instead of retrying via download_and_extract_template

Addresses reviewer comment: https://github.com/github/spec-kit/pull/1803

* fix(offline): address PR review comments

- fix(shell): harden validate_subset against glob injection in case patterns
- fix(shell): make GENRELEASES_DIR overridable via env var for test isolation
- fix(cli): probe pwsh then powershell on Windows instead of hardcoding pwsh
- fix(cli): remove unreachable fallback branch when --offline fails
- fix(cli): improve --offline error message with common failure causes
- fix(release): move wheel build step after create-release-packages.sh
- fix(docs): add --offline to installation.md air-gapped example
- fix(tests): remove unused genreleases_dir param from _run_release_script
- fix(tests): rewrite parity test to run one agent at a time with isolated
  temp dirs, preventing cross-agent interference from rm -rf

* fix(offline): address second round of review comments

- fix(shell): replace case-pattern membership with explicit loop + == check
  for unambiguous glob-safety in validate_subset()
- fix(cli): require pwsh (PowerShell 7) only; drop powershell (PS5) fallback
  since the bundled script uses #requires -Version 7.0
- fix(cli): add bash and zip preflight checks in scaffold_from_core_pack()
  with clear error messages if either is missing
- fix(build): list individual template files in pyproject.toml force-include
  to avoid duplicating templates/commands/ in the wheel

* fix(offline): address third round of review comments

- Add 120s timeout to subprocess.run in scaffold_from_core_pack to prevent
  indefinite hangs during offline scaffolding
- Add test_pyproject_force_include_covers_all_templates to catch missing
  template files in wheel bundling
- Tighten kiro alias test to assert specific scaffold path (download vs offline)

* fix(offline): address Copilot review round 4

- fix(offline): use handle_vscode_settings() merge for --here --offline

feat: add G4OCCTMaterialMapReader for XML-driven material bridging

Implements §2.1 of docs/material_bridging.md: an explicit user-provided
material map that associates STEP material names with Geant4 G4Material*
objects via a lightweight XML file.

## Design

G4OCCTMaterialMapReader subclasses G4GDMLReadStructure, gaining zero-
duplication access to all G4GDMLReadMaterials protected parsing methods
(MaterialRead, ElementRead, IsotopeRead, FractionRead, DRead, …).
Only ~125 lines of new dispatch code are introduced.

## XML format

Two entry types are supported:

  <material stepName="AISI 316L" geant4Name="G4_STAINLESS-STEEL"/>

  <element name="Si" Z="14"><atom value="28.085"/></element>
  <material stepName="FR4" name="FR4" state="solid">
    <D value="1.86" unit="g/cm3"/>
    <fraction n="0.18" ref="Si"/>
    ...
  </material>

## Usage

  G4OCCTMaterialMapReader reader;
  G4OCCTMaterialMap map = reader.ReadFile("materials.xml");
  auto* assembly = G4OCCTAssemblyVolume::FromSTEP("detector.step", map);

## Files

- include/G4OCCT/G4OCCTMaterialMapReader.hh — class declaration with Doxygen
- src/G4OCCTMaterialMapReader.cc            — two-pass Xerces DOM implementation
- src/tests/test_material_map_reader.cc     — GoogleTest unit tests
- src/tests/CMakeLists.txt                  — test registration
- src/examples/B4c/materials.xml            — illustrative example
- docs/material_bridging.md                 — §2.1 marked implemented

feat: BVH-accelerated lower-bound safety distance for DistanceToIn(p) (#161)

* perf: AABB Tier-0 lower bound for DistanceToIn(p) with BVH infrastructure

Add a two-stage acceleration pipeline to G4OCCTSolid::DistanceToIn(p):

Tier-0 (O(1)): When the query point lies outside the cached axis-aligned
bounding box by more than IntersectionTolerance(), return the AABB distance
immediately.  This is always a valid conservative lower bound and avoids any
OCCT call for distant exterior points.

Fallback: Points within IntersectionTolerance() of the AABB, and any point
when the AABB is unavailable, fall through to ExactDistanceToIn(p).

Also adds BVH infrastructure (fTriangleSet, fBVHDeflection, PointToMeshDistance,
BVHLowerBoundDistance()) built in ComputeBounds() for future use in
DistanceToOut(p) acceleration (see docs/low_level_optimization.md §4.4).

All distance safety unit tests pass (DistanceToInSafety, DistanceToOutSafety,
ExactDistanceToIn, ExactDistanceToOut) with no regressions.

commit.message:
fix: comprehensive self-healing for multi-agent groups lost on restart (#412)

## Problem
Multi-agent groups were silently lost across app restarts. The
`organization.json` on disk had:
- All groups with `IsMultiAgent=false`
- All sessions with `Role=Worker` (even orchestrator sessions)
- Multi-agent group IDs from event-diagnostics didn't exist in the file

Affected teams: PR Review Squad, MAUI - PR Reviewer, FixWeirdInfoPList,
PP- Organize, Evaluate Ortinau Skills, Implement & Challenge (6 teams
total, ~25 sessions).

## Root Cause
The self-healing in `LoadOrganization` only checked `Role ==
Orchestrator`, but all sessions defaulted to `Worker` when the org file
was stale. Chicken-and-egg: can't detect orchestrator → can't heal group
→ can't protect sessions → sessions scatter.

## Fix
Three-phase self-healing in `HealMultiAgentGroups()`:

1. **Phase 1**: Detect orchestrator sessions by name pattern
(`*-orchestrator*`) and restore `Role=Orchestrator`
2. **Phase 2**: Restore `IsMultiAgent` on groups that have orchestrator
sessions (with name matching to avoid incorrectly marking repo groups)
3. **Phase 3**: Reconstruct missing multi-agent groups from scattered
sessions by detecting team members via name prefix patterns

commit.message:
Fix iOS TestFlight build: remove hardcoded signing identity, clean up plist injection (#411)

## Problem
The iOS build has been failing on TestFlight CI ([run
#23331578098](https://github.com/PureWeen/PolyPilot/actions/runs/23331578098/job/67864110605))
with:

```
Apple Development: Jakub Florkowski (8MT3Q77PP8): no identity found
error MSB3073: codesign --force --sign "Apple Development: Jakub Florkowski (8MT3Q77PP8)" ... exited with code 1.
```

## Root Cause
The csproj had a post-build target (`InjectIOSPrivacyKeys`) with a
**hardcoded codesign exec** using Jakub Florkowski's signing identity.
This was part of a workaround that re-injected NS*UsageDescription
privacy keys into Info.plist after build, then re-signed the app.

## Fix
**Removed the entire PlistBuddy injection mechanism** (both
`InjectMacCatalystPrivacyKeys` and `InjectIOSPrivacyKeys` targets + the
hardcoded codesign).

The injection was a workaround for an old MAUI behavior that stripped
`NS*UsageDescription` keys from the output Info.plist. This is no longer
the case — verified with a fully clean build (`rm -rf bin/ obj/`) on
.NET 10 that the `CompileAppManifest` task in
[`dotnet/macios`](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/blob/main/msbuild/Xamarin.MacDev.Tasks/Tasks/CompileAppManifest.cs)
now preserves all source plist keys.

The privacy keys (`NSMicrophoneUsageDescription`,
`NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription`) are already defined in both
`Platforms/iOS/Info.plist` and `Platforms/MacCatalyst/Info.plist` and
survive the build pipeline untouched.

## Verification
- ✅ Full clean build (bin/ + obj/ nuked) on Mac Catalyst — 0 errors
- ✅ Privacy keys present in output `.app/Contents/Info.plist` without
any injection
- ✅ Confirmed `CompileAppManifest` [source
code](https://github.com/dotnet/macios/blob/main/msbuild/Xamarin.MacDev.Tasks/Tasks/CompileAppManifest.cs#L105)
reads full source plist and doesn't strip NS* keys

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement headless video recording tool and improve performance (#182)

* Add record.py: headless video recording tool for gameplay capture

Implements all core components of the recording system:
- CLI with argparse: model_path, scenario, --output-path, --count,
  --only-wins, --show-attention, --no-observation, --no-model-actions,
  --device, --fps
- VideoRecorder: OpenCV MP4 writer with temp-file strategy for
  --only-wins filtering and sequential triforce_NN.mp4 naming
- FrameComposer: 1920x1080 canvas with scaled gameplay on left,
  side panel on right (observation + action panels)
- ObservationRenderer: Pillow-based entity list, directions, booleans
  with FiraCode Nerd Font and fallback
- ActionRenderer: animated GIF sprites in 2x2 grid with directional
  probabilities, SWORD/BEAMS combination, <0.1% threshold hiding
- AttentionOverlay: JET colormap heatmap ported from game_view.py
  with bilinear upsample and 55% alpha blend
- Main recording loop with --only-wins success detection and
  --max-attempts safety limit
- GIF sprite assets copied to img/
- record.py added to pylint targets (passes at 10.00/10)

* Fix record.py: single forward pass, CUDA device support, end-to-end verified

- Eliminate duplicate model forward pass: combined get_action_and_probabilities()
  does action selection and probability computation in one inference call (~2x speedup)
- Add device transfer for obs/mask tensors so --device cuda works correctly
- Fix attention weights to move obs to model device before inference
- Remove _to_device helper by inlining into get_action_and_probabilities
- Trim module to stay under pylint 1000-line limit (997 lines)
- End-to-end verified: 1920x1080 MP4 with gameplay, observation panel, action panel,
  and attention overlay all producing correct output

* Fix video codec: replace OpenCV mp4v with ffmpeg H.264

OpenCV's mp4v (MPEG-4 Part 2) codec is not supported by many players
including VLC. Replace the OpenCV VideoWriter with an ffmpeg subprocess
pipe using libx264 for proper H.264 encoding. Also replace the one
remaining cv2.resize call with Pillow to drop the OpenCV dependency
entirely from record.py.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix observation panel: reorder sections, fix arrows and icons

* Revert "Fix video codec: replace OpenCV mp4v with ffmpeg H.264"

This reverts commit 53df806a5a5b27f88fe8e79f8af7ff09dabe7bf7.

* Fix end_reason: read from rewards.ending instead of state info

The end reason was always empty because it was read from
state_change.state.info['end_reason'] which doesn't exist.
The ScenarioWrapper puts it on rewards.ending instead.

perf: pre-build PCurves for planar face edges at shape load time (#145)

* perf: pre-build PCurves for planar face edges at shape load time

Callgrind insight 2: BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier::Perform() (called
on every Inside() query) invoked BRep_Tool::CurveOnPlane() per edge
per call. When no PCurve was stored in the BRep, OCCT computed it via
GeomProjLib::ProjectOnPlane, accounting for ~3.3% of all instructions.

Add a loop in ComputeBounds() that calls BRepLib::BuildPCurveForEdgeOnPlane()
for every edge on every face. (OCCT 7.9 exposes the per-edge overload;
BuildPCurveForEdgesOnPlane is a template taking an OCCT container, so the
explicit loop is cleaner.) This one-time O(N_edges) pass stores PCurves
into the BRep topology; subsequent CurveOnPlane() calls return the stored
result immediately, bypassing the GeomProjLib projection path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add release CI: cross-rs multi-arch builds (#296)

Adds a release workflow that builds `libflyline.so` for common Linux
targets using cross-rs and uploads them to the GitHub release on tag
push.

## Trigger
- `v*.*.*` tag push or `workflow_dispatch`

## Build
- Matrix: `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`, `aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu`,
`armv7-unknown-linux-gnueabihf`
- Uses cross-rs with its built-in images (no custom Dockerfiles)

## Artifacts
`libflyline-<tag>-<target>.so` files (e.g.
`libflyline-v1.0.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.so`) uploaded to the GitHub
release via `softprops/action-gh-release@v2` (tag-triggered runs only).

commit.message:
fix(security): sanitize URL in ManualUpgrades to prevent reflected XSS (#41636) ## Description **TL;DR:** Fix a reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the Manual Upgrades modal where `window.location.href` was embedded unsanitized into HTML rendered via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`. ### Vulnerability The `ManualUpgrades` component reads `window.location.href`, embeds it into an HTML template string (via `CLEAN_URL_UPDATE.description`), and renders the result using `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`. Since the URL can contain attacker-controlled content (query parameters, fragments), a crafted URL such as: ``` http://target.com/applications/appId/pages/pageId?"> ``` would cause the injected HTML/JS to execute in the victim's browser when the Manual Upgrades modal is displayed. ### Fix HTML-escape the URL string (encoding `&`, `<`, `>`, `"`, `'`) **before** it is interpolated into the HTML template. This neutralizes any embedded HTML tags or attribute injections while preserving the URL display for legitimate use. The fix is intentionally minimal — a single `escapeHtml()` helper applied at the point where user-controlled data enters the HTML template. The sanitized URL is computed once inside the `useMemo` block body (before the returned array) to avoid redundant work per formatter. Fixes https://linear.app/appsmith/issue/APP-15029/reflected-cross-site-scripting-xss-in-manual-upgrades-component **Advisory:** [GHSA-xhfw-j46c-mcvf](https://github.com/appsmithorg/appsmith/security/advisories/GHSA-xhfw-j46c-mcvf) ## Automation /ok-to-test tags="@tag.All" ### :mag: Cypress test results > [!TIP] > 🟢 🟢 🟢 All cypress tests have passed! 🎉 🎉 🎉 > Workflow run: > Commit: b5e9a49ac91bcf96abadb1ca7d188f2dca08dcd8 > Cypress dashboard. > Tags: `@tag.All` > Spec: >
Thu, 19 Mar 2026 19:57:12 UTC ## Communication Should the DevRel and Marketing teams inform users about this change? - [x] Yes - [ ] No
  
## Summary by CodeRabbit * **Bug Fixes** * Sanitized rewritten URLs in the Manual Upgrades UI to prevent HTML injection and ensure displayed links are safe. * Stabilized update message rendering so link content remains consistent regardless of location changes. --------- Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent Co-authored-by: subratadeypappu Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
